name: Communication Studies
description: An elective course exploring the theory and practice of human communication across interpersonal, group, organizational, and mass media contexts.

modules.title: Communication Process & Models
modules.objectives: Identify the key elements of the communication process; compare and contrast linear, interactive, and transactional models of communication.
modules.readingNote: Communication in the Real World (UMN OER) — Ch 1.2: The Communication Process
modules.practiceNote: Apply the transactional model by mapping a real conversation onto its elements
modules.assignment: Draw and label a transactional model of communication using a real conversation you had this week. Identify sender, receiver, message, channel, feedback, noise, and context. Write a 250-word reflection explaining how noise (physical or psychological) affected the exchange.

modules.title: Verbal Communication
modules.objectives: Explain how language creates meaning; analyze the impact of word choice, clarity, and language style on effective verbal communication.
modules.readingNote: Communication in the Real World (UMN OER) — Ch 3.1: Language and Meaning
modules.video2Note: YouTube — Power of Words: Verbal Communication Skills
modules.practiceNote: Practice rewriting vague sentences using precise, inclusive, and clear language
modules.assignment: Collect five examples of ambiguous or misleading verbal messages from news headlines, advertisements, or social media. For each, rewrite the message to be clear and unambiguous, and explain in 50 words per example what made the original unclear.

modules.title: Nonverbal Communication
modules.objectives: Identify categories of nonverbal communication; analyze how body language, proxemics, and paralanguage convey meaning alongside verbal messages.
modules.readingNote: Communication in the Real World (UMN OER) — Ch 4.1: Principles and Functions of Nonverbal Communication
modules.video2Note: YouTube — Your Body Language May Shape Who You Are (TED)
modules.practiceNote: Identify nonverbal cues in a silent video clip and explain their communicative function
modules.assignment: Watch a 5-minute segment of a TV show or film with the sound muted. Record at least 10 distinct nonverbal signals you observe (facial expressions, gestures, proxemics, touch, etc.) and write a 300-word analysis of what story the nonverbal communication tells without words.

modules.title: Listening & Feedback
modules.objectives: Distinguish between hearing and active listening; apply effective feedback strategies to improve communication outcomes.
modules.readingNote: Communication in the Real World (UMN OER) — Ch 5.1: Understanding How and Why We Listen
modules.practiceNote: Practice the HURIER model steps using a recorded listening exercise
modules.assignment: Conduct a 10-minute conversation with a family member or friend on a topic they care about, using only active listening techniques (paraphrasing, open questions, minimal encouragers). Immediately after, write a 300-word reflection on what made active listening difficult, what you learned about the speaker, and how feedback shaped the conversation.

modules.title: Intrapersonal Communication
modules.objectives: Define intrapersonal communication and explain its role in self-concept, self-esteem, and internal dialogue.
modules.readingNote: Communication in the Real World (UMN OER) — Ch 2.1: Self-Concept
modules.video2Note: YouTube — The Power of Intrapersonal Communication
modules.practiceNote: Complete a self-concept inventory and reflect on how internal messages shape behavior
modules.assignment: Keep a 3-day intrapersonal journal: each day, record at least three instances of your internal self-talk (positive, negative, or neutral). At the end of the three days, write a 350-word analysis identifying patterns in your internal dialogue and how those messages influenced your communication with others.

modules.title: Interpersonal Communication
modules.objectives: Apply principles of interpersonal communication to build, maintain, and repair relationships; explain disclosure and reciprocity.
modules.readingNote: Communication in the Real World (UMN OER) — Ch 6.1: Principles of Interpersonal Communication
modules.video2Note: YouTube — What Makes a Good Conversation (TED)
modules.practiceNote: Practice the Johari Window self-disclosure exercise with a partner
modules.assignment: Map your three closest relationships using the Johari Window model. For each relationship, identify what information sits in each quadrant (open, hidden, blind, unknown) and write a 300-word explanation of how varying levels of self-disclosure have shaped each relationship.

modules.title: Small Group Communication
modules.objectives: Identify stages of group development and member roles; analyze factors that contribute to or detract from group effectiveness.
modules.readingNote: Communication in the Real World (UMN OER) — Ch 13.1: Understanding Small Groups
modules.video2Note: YouTube — Tuckman's Stages of Group Development
modules.practiceNote: Analyze a case study group using Tuckman's five stages model
modules.assignment: Think of a group project or team experience you have been part of (school, sport, club). Write a 400-word analysis applying Tuckman's stages (Forming, Storming, Norming, Performing, Adjourning) to that group, identifying at least two positive and two negative communication behaviors you observed.

modules.title: Organizational Communication
modules.objectives: Explain formal and informal communication flows within organizations; evaluate the impact of organizational culture on communication.
modules.readingNote: Communication in the Real World (UMN OER) — Ch 14.1: Organizational Communication
modules.video2Note: YouTube — Organizational Communication: Formal and Informal Channels
modules.practiceNote: Create a communication flow diagram for a real or hypothetical organization
modules.assignment: Interview someone who works in an organization (parent, neighbor, community member) about how information flows in their workplace. Ask about formal channels (meetings, memos, email) and informal channels (grapevine, watercooler). Write a 350-word report comparing what they describe to the concepts of downward, upward, and horizontal communication.

quizQuestions.question: Which model of communication depicts communication as a two-way process with feedback?
quizQuestions.explanation: The interactive model adds feedback to the linear model, making communication a two-way exchange rather than a one-way transmission.

quizQuestions.question: In the communication process, 'noise' refers to:
options: Only loud sounds that interrupt speech
Any interference that distorts the message
The tone of voice used by the sender
The medium through which a message travels
quizQuestions.explanation: Noise is any physical, psychological, or semantic interference that distorts or disrupts the accurate transmission and reception of a message.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "Which model of communication depicts communication as a two-way process with feedback" is: B.
quizQuestions.explanation: True. B is correct.

quizQuestions.question: Fill in the blank: Complete: In the context of the topic, ___ refers to "the original linear model of communication was developed by:."
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: A.

quizQuestions.question: The study of the relationship between language and meaning is called:
quizQuestions.explanation: Semantics is the branch of linguistics concerned with meaning — how words and sentences carry and convey meaning.

quizQuestions.question: Which type of language refers to the emotional associations a word carries beyond its dictionary definition?
quizQuestions.explanation: Connotative meaning includes the emotional, cultural, or personal associations attached to a word, beyond its literal denotative definition.

quizQuestions.question: Using vague or abstract words that can be interpreted in multiple ways is called using:
quizQuestions.explanation: Equivocal language consists of words or phrases that have more than one commonly accepted meaning, which can cause misunderstanding.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "The study of the relationship between language and meaning is called:" is: C.
quizQuestions.explanation: True. C is correct.

quizQuestions.question: Albert Mehrabian's research suggested that in face-to-face communication, the majority of emotional meaning is conveyed through:
options: Tone of voice and body language
quizQuestions.explanation: Mehrabian's oft-cited (though frequently overgeneralized) research suggested that tone of voice and body language carry the bulk of emotional meaning in face-to-face interactions.

quizQuestions.question: The study of personal space and how people use physical distance in communication is called:
quizQuestions.explanation: Proxemics, developed by Edward Hall, is the study of how people perceive and use space in communication, including intimate, personal, social, and public zones.

quizQuestions.question: Which of the following is an example of paralanguage?
options: A gesture that replaces a spoken word
The rate, pitch, and volume of speech
quizQuestions.explanation: Paralanguage refers to vocal elements that accompany spoken words, including rate, pitch, volume, and intonation, but not the words themselves.

quizQuestions.question: Fill in the blank: Complete: In the context of the topic, ___ refers to "nonverbal communication that manages the flow of conversation (e.g., nodding to indicate 'keep talking') is called:."
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: B.

quizQuestions.question: Which of the following best describes active listening?
options: Hearing the words spoken while multitasking
Fully concentrating on, understanding, and responding to a speaker
Waiting silently until the speaker finishes to plan your reply
Agreeing with everything the speaker says
quizQuestions.explanation: Active listening involves full attention, understanding, and engaged response to the speaker, going beyond mere hearing to genuine comprehension.

quizQuestions.question: Paraphrasing in active listening means:
options: Repeating the speaker's exact words back to them
Restating the speaker's message in your own words to confirm understanding
Asking a new question to change the topic
Summarizing only the emotional content of the message
quizQuestions.explanation: Paraphrasing involves restating the speaker's message in your own words to confirm you understood correctly and show you were paying attention.

quizQuestions.question: Which listening barrier involves mentally preparing your response while the other person is still speaking?
quizQuestions.explanation: Rehearsal is the barrier where the listener is mentally composing a reply rather than fully attending to what the speaker is saying.

quizQuestions.question: Evaluative feedback in communication is best described as feedback that:
options: Judges or assesses the worth of the message or the speaker
Asks for clarification about the message
Reflects the speaker's emotions back to them
quizQuestions.explanation: Evaluative feedback makes a judgment about the message or person (e.g., 'That was a bad idea'), as opposed to descriptive or empathic feedback.

quizQuestions.question: Intrapersonal communication refers to:
options: Communication that occurs within one's own mind
quizQuestions.explanation: Intrapersonal communication is the internal dialogue and self-talk that occurs within an individual's own mind.

quizQuestions.question: Self-concept is best defined as:
options: Your overall subjective evaluation of your own worth
The mental image you hold of yourself
quizQuestions.explanation: Self-concept is the mental image or perception an individual holds of themselves, encompassing beliefs about one's personality, abilities, and roles.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "Intrapersonal communication refers to:" is: B.
quizQuestions.explanation: True. B is correct.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "Self-concept is best defined as:" is: How others perceive you.
quizQuestions.explanation: False. The correct answer is: C.

quizQuestions.question: The Johari Window model helps individuals understand:
options: How messages are encoded and decoded
The relationship between self-disclosure and mutual awareness
quizQuestions.explanation: The Johari Window is a tool for understanding self-disclosure and interpersonal awareness, depicting information known/unknown to self and others in four quadrants.

quizQuestions.question: Self-disclosure in interpersonal communication refers to:
options: Revealing information about yourself to others
Listening carefully to what another person says
Keeping personal information private in professional settings
Offering feedback on another person's behavior
quizQuestions.explanation: Self-disclosure is the intentional act of sharing personal information — thoughts, feelings, or experiences — with another person.

quizQuestions.question: Social Penetration Theory, developed by Altman and Taylor, describes relationships as progressing through:
options: Five fixed stages that always occur in order
Increasing breadth and depth of self-disclosure over time
quizQuestions.explanation: Social Penetration Theory uses an 'onion' metaphor, proposing that relationships develop as self-disclosure increases in both breadth (topics) and depth (intimacy).

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "The Johari Window model helps individuals understand:" is: B.
quizQuestions.explanation: True. B is correct.

quizQuestions.question: Fill in the blank: Complete: In the context of the topic, ___ refers to "which of the following best describes a relationship at the 'stable exchange' stage of social penetration theory."
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: B.

quizQuestions.question: According to Tuckman's model, in which stage do group members first experience conflict and power struggles?
quizQuestions.explanation: In the Storming stage, group members begin to assert ideas and compete for positions, leading to interpersonal conflict and tension before norms are established.

quizQuestions.question: A 'task role' in small group communication is best described as a role that:
options: Maintains emotional harmony within the group
Helps the group achieve its goals and complete work
Promotes individual interests over group goals
Provides social support to discouraged members
quizQuestions.explanation: Task roles (e.g., initiator, information seeker, clarifier) are behaviors that help the group accomplish its assigned work and reach its objectives.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "According to Tuckman's model, in which stage do group members first experience conflict and power struggles" is: B.
quizQuestions.explanation: True. B is correct.

quizQuestions.question: Fill in the blank: Complete: In the context of the topic, ___ refers to "in tuckman's model, the norming stage is characterized by:."
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: C.

quizQuestions.question: Downward communication in an organization flows:
options: Between departments at the same level
From the organization to external stakeholders
quizQuestions.explanation: Downward communication flows from higher levels of the organizational hierarchy (managers) to lower levels (subordinates), conveying instructions and policies.

quizQuestions.question: The 'grapevine' in organizational communication refers to:
options: Official memos circulated through management channels
A visual chart of the organization's structure
A formal process for filing employee grievances
quizQuestions.explanation: The organizational grapevine is the informal, unofficial network of communication among employees that travels outside of formal channels.

quizQuestions.question: Horizontal communication in an organization refers to communication:
options: Between people at the same hierarchical level
Between the organization and the public
quizQuestions.explanation: Horizontal (or lateral) communication occurs between employees, departments, or teams at the same level of the organizational hierarchy.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "Downward communication in an organization flows:" is: B.
quizQuestions.explanation: True. B is correct.

questions.question: Which communication model is most realistic because it shows both parties simultaneously sending and receiving messages?
questions.explanation: The transactional model reflects the simultaneous, mutually influential nature of real communication, where all parties act as senders and receivers at the same time.

questions.question: Semantic noise is best described as:
options: Loud environmental sounds that interrupt speech
Differences in word meaning that cause misunderstanding
Physical distractions in the communication environment
questions.explanation: Semantic noise occurs when words or symbols mean different things to the sender and receiver, causing the message to be misinterpreted.

questions.question: The difference between denotative and connotative meaning is that:
options: Denotative meaning is emotional; connotative meaning is literal
Denotative meaning is the dictionary definition; connotative meaning includes emotional associations
Connotative meaning is precise; denotative meaning is vague
They refer to the same concept from different communication traditions
questions.explanation: Denotative meaning is the objective, dictionary definition of a word; connotative meaning includes the subjective, emotional, and cultural associations the word carries.

questions.question: True or False: The answer to "Proxemics is the study of how people use:" is: B.
questions.explanation: True. B is correct.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a characteristic of active listening?
options: Maintaining eye contact with the speaker
Internally planning your response while the speaker talks
questions.explanation: Planning your response while someone is talking (rehearsal) is a barrier to active listening, not a characteristic of it; active listeners focus fully on the speaker.

questions.question: The 'looking-glass self' theory suggests that self-concept develops through:
options: Comparison with idealized images in media
Imagining how others perceive and evaluate us
Childhood experiences with parents and authority figures
questions.explanation: Charles Cooley's looking-glass self theory proposes that individuals form self-concept based on how they imagine they appear to others and how they imagine others judge them.

questions.question: The Johari Window quadrant labeled 'Hidden Area' contains information that:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: B.

questions.question: True or False: The answer to "Social Penetration Theory uses an onion metaphor to illustrate that relationships deepen through:" is: Conflict and resolution cycles.
questions.explanation: False. The correct answer is: B.

questions.question: In which stage of Tuckman's group development model does the group reach its highest level of productivity?
questions.explanation: The Performing stage is when the group has resolved interpersonal issues, established roles, and is functioning at peak effectiveness toward its goals.

questions.question: Groupthink is most likely to occur in groups that are:
options: Highly cohesive and under pressure to reach quick consensus
Made up of members with low investment in outcomes
Led by a leader who actively encourages dissent
questions.explanation: Groupthink is most common in highly cohesive groups facing pressure, where maintaining harmony becomes more important than evaluating alternatives critically.

questions.question: Horizontal communication within an organization flows between:
options: Departments or individuals at the same hierarchical level
questions.explanation: Horizontal or lateral communication moves between peers — departments or employees at the same level — coordinating efforts across the organization.

questions.question: True or False: The answer to "Which type of communication behavior would be classified as an 'informal channel' in organizational communication" is: C.
questions.explanation: True. C is correct.

questions.question: In high-context cultures, communication tends to rely primarily on:
options: Explicit verbal statements and written contracts
Implicit understanding, context, and nonverbal signals
Precise technical language and formal vocabulary
Direct confrontation and clear expression of disagreement
questions.explanation: High-context cultures depend on shared context, relationships, and nonverbal cues to convey meaning, with less reliance on explicit verbal messages.

questions.question: Hofstede's individualism dimension is characterized by cultures that emphasize:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: B.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of an emblem in nonverbal communication?
options: Using hand gestures to emphasize a spoken point
Giving a thumbs-up to mean 'OK' or 'good'
Adjusting your posture when the topic becomes serious
questions.explanation: Emblems are gestures with specific, culturally shared verbal translations that can stand alone without spoken words, such as the thumbs-up for approval.

questions.question: True or False: The answer to "The Shannon-Weaver model of communication was originally designed to model:" is: Human face-to-face conversation.
questions.explanation: False. The correct answer is: B.

questions.question: Abstract language is problematic in communication because it:
options: Is too technical for most audiences to understand
Lacks specificity and can be interpreted in multiple ways
Carries strong negative connotations for listeners
Relies too heavily on nonverbal cues for meaning
questions.explanation: Abstract language (e.g., 'improve performance' vs. 'submit reports by 5 PM') lacks the specific referents needed for a shared, unambiguous meaning.

questions.question: Kinesics is the study of:
options: The use of physical space in communication
Body movement and gesture as communication
The meaning of touch in social interaction
questions.explanation: Kinesics is the study of body language — gestures, facial expressions, eye contact, and posture — as forms of nonverbal communication.

questions.question: Which listening style involves focusing exclusively on facts and data while filtering out emotional content?
questions.explanation: Analytical (or critical) listening focuses on evaluating and analyzing the informational content of a message, often setting aside emotional dimensions.

questions.question: True or False: The answer to "Cultivation Theory, developed by George Gerbner, proposes that:" is: A.
questions.explanation: True. A is correct.

questions.question: Agenda-setting theory differs from cultivation theory in that agenda-setting focuses on:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: B.

questions.question: Filter bubbles in digital communication refer to:
options: Privacy settings that hide personal data from advertisers
Algorithm-driven information environments where users see only content aligned with their views
Content moderation systems that remove harmful material
Tools that allow users to block unwanted communications
questions.explanation: Filter bubbles are the result of personalization algorithms that curate content matching users' preferences, limiting exposure to diverse perspectives.

questions.question: Which of Cialdini's six principles of persuasion involves giving something first to create an obligation to reciprocate?
questions.explanation: Cialdini's reciprocity principle states that people feel obligated to return favors; giving a free sample or gift triggers a felt obligation to reciprocate.

questions.question: True or False: The answer to "In Fisher and Ury's principled negotiation, 'inventing options for mutual gain' means:" is: Finding the midpoint between two competing demands.
questions.explanation: False. The correct answer is: B.

questions.question: Which conflict management style involves both parties giving up something to reach a middle-ground solution?
questions.explanation: Compromising involves each party making concessions to reach a mutually acceptable middle-ground solution, unlike collaborating which seeks a fully satisfying win-win.

questions.question: The NCA Credo for Ethical Communication asserts that ethical communicators:
options: Advocate for free expression while respecting others' dignity
Keep all information confidential unless legally required to disclose it
Adapt messages to maximize persuasive impact regardless of accuracy
questions.explanation: The NCA Credo holds that ethical communicators champion free speech while treating others with dignity, sharing accurate information, and acknowledging diverse viewpoints.

questions.question: Intercultural communication competence requires which of the following?
options: Eliminating your cultural identity when communicating across cultures
Knowledge, motivation, and skill to communicate effectively across cultural differences
Speaking the native language of every culture you encounter
Adopting the norms of the dominant culture in any interaction
questions.explanation: Intercultural communication competence involves the knowledge of cultural differences, the motivation to engage across cultures, and the skill to adapt communication appropriately.

questions.question: Parasocial relationships are best described as:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: B.

questions.question: Which of the following best describes the Central Route in the Elaboration Likelihood Model?
options: Persuasion that relies on superficial cues like the speaker's attractiveness
Persuasion achieved through careful consideration of the quality of arguments
Persuasion through repeated exposure to a message without conscious processing
Persuasion achieved by creating fear or anxiety in the audience
questions.explanation: The Central Route involves high elaboration — the audience carefully processes and evaluates the quality of the arguments presented, leading to lasting attitude change.

questions.question: Organizational culture is MOST directly communicated through:
options: Stories, rituals, symbols, and informal norms within the workplace
The official organizational chart and job descriptions
questions.explanation: Organizational culture is primarily communicated and maintained through informal channels: stories, rituals, symbols, and the unwritten norms that shape daily behavior.

questions.question: The 'uses and gratifications' framework views media audiences as:
options: Passive recipients of media messages with no choice in consumption
Active selectors of media based on personal needs and goals
Victims of media manipulation and propaganda
Homogeneous groups who respond identically to media content
questions.explanation: Uses and Gratifications Theory portrays audiences as active agents who choose media based on specific needs (information, entertainment, social connection, identity).

questions.question: True or False: The answer to "Which stage of Tuckman's model involves the group disbanding after achieving its goal" is: Performing.
questions.explanation: False. The correct answer is: C.

questions.question: The digital divide is primarily a concern because it:
options: Creates too many choices for consumers
Creates unequal access to information, education, and economic opportunity
Slows the pace of technological innovation
Encourages overuse of digital devices among young people
questions.explanation: The digital divide creates significant inequalities as those without internet or device access are excluded from educational, economic, and social opportunities that are increasingly digital.

questions.question: In communication ethics, the principle of non-maleficence means communicators should:
options: Always tell the complete truth even if harmful
Avoid causing unnecessary harm through their communication
Prioritize the interests of the majority over the minority
Share information only when legally required
questions.explanation: Non-maleficence (do no harm) in communication ethics requires that communicators avoid messages or practices that unnecessarily harm individuals, communities, or society.

questions.question: Framing in mass media communication refers to:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: B.
